instruction: Read each passage carefully and answer the questions that follow. Answers should be based only on the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Gerrard was a playwright who lived alone in a quiet cottage in the countryside. His peaceful life suddenly changed when an armed intruder entered his house. The man was a criminal hiding from the police.
questions: What was Gerrard’s profession?
Where did Gerrard live?
Who entered Gerrard’s house?
Why had the intruder come there?
What kind of life did Gerrard live before the incident?

passages.paragraph: The intruder carried a revolver and threatened Gerrard. He told Gerrard that he had already killed a policeman and was running away from the law. The intruder planned to kill Gerrard and take his identity.
questions: What weapon did the intruder carry?
Whom had the intruder killed earlier?
Why was the intruder hiding from the police?
What was the intruder’s plan?
Why did he want Gerrard’s identity?

passages.paragraph: Instead of becoming frightened, Gerrard remained calm. He started talking cleverly to the intruder. His calm behaviour surprised the criminal, who expected Gerrard to panic.
questions: How did Gerrard react to the threat?
What surprised the intruder?
Why did the intruder expect Gerrard to panic?
What quality did Gerrard show?
How did Gerrard begin to handle the situation?

passages.paragraph: Gerrard told the intruder that killing him would not help him escape the police. He said that he himself was a criminal who had been hiding from the law for years.
questions: What did Gerrard claim about himself?
Why did Gerrard say killing him was useless?
How long did Gerrard claim he had been hiding?
Why did Gerrard tell such a story?
What effect did this story have on the intruder?

passages.paragraph: The intruder became confused after hearing Gerrard’s story. Gerrard continued speaking confidently and cleverly. Slowly, he gained control of the conversation.
questions: How did the intruder react to Gerrard’s story?
What quality did Gerrard show while speaking?
How did Gerrard gain control of the situation?
What was the intruder feeling at that moment?
Why did Gerrard continue the conversation?

passages.paragraph: Gerrard suggested that the intruder should leave the cottage with him. He explained that it would be safer for both of them. The intruder began to believe Gerrard’s story.
questions: What suggestion did Gerrard make?
Why did Gerrard say they should leave together?
Did the intruder believe Gerrard?
What mistake did the intruder make?
What was Gerrard secretly planning?

passages.paragraph: When the intruder moved closer, Gerrard suddenly pushed him into a cupboard. The revolver fell from the intruder’s hand. Gerrard quickly locked the cupboard door.
questions: What did Gerrard do suddenly?
Where was the intruder trapped?
What happened to the revolver?
How did Gerrard secure the situation?
Why was this moment important?

passages.paragraph: After trapping the intruder, Gerrard picked up the revolver. He then telephoned the police to inform them about the criminal.
questions: What did Gerrard do after trapping the intruder?
Whom did Gerrard call?
Why did he call the police?
What did the revolver represent at that moment?
How did Gerrard ensure justice?

passages.paragraph: The intruder’s plan failed because he underestimated Gerrard. He believed Gerrard would be frightened and helpless, but Gerrard proved to be intelligent and courageous.
questions: Why did the intruder’s plan fail?
What mistake did the intruder make?
What qualities did Gerrard show?
How did Gerrard prove the intruder wrong?
What lesson can be learned from this situation?

passages.paragraph: The play highlights the importance of presence of mind. Gerrard’s intelligence and calm thinking helped him overcome a dangerous situation without violence.
questions: What quality helped Gerrard overcome danger?
Did Gerrard use violence to defeat the intruder?
What message does the play convey?
Why is calm thinking important in danger?
What lesson does the play teach readers?
